title: ASV External Scan Failure and Non-Compliant Finding Trend

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 11.3.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 11.3.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.73: ASV External Scan Failure and Non-Compliant Finding Trend.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.73' running on index=vm qualys:was or tenable:io:vm with scan_type="ASV" if tagged, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060)
spl:
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:was" earliest=-120d
| where match(scan_name,"(?i)ASV|PCI\\s*External)")
| eval failed_scan=if(match(status,"(?i)error|failed|aborted"),1,0)
| timechart span=30d sum(failed_scan) as failed_runs, count as total_runs
| eval fail_pct=if(total_runs>0, round(100*failed_runs/total_runs,2), null())
description: Tracks approved scanning vendor run outcomes and residual high-risk findings on Internet-facing payment hosts so quarterly external vulnerability scanning evidence stays current and remediated.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vm and sourcetypes qualys:was. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Tag ASV scans distinctly from internal VM. (2) Join open findings `severity>=3` on same `fqdn`. (3) Alert if `fail_pct>0` two quarters in a row. (4) Store PDF reports in GRC with Splunk deep link. Indexes required: `index=vm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:was`.
visualization: Line (`fail_pct`), column (failed_runs), table (last scan status by fqdn).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Scan failures during legacy system lifecycle, documented compensating controls, or planned upgrade windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` `qualys:was` or `tenable:io:vm` with `scan_type="ASV"` if tagged.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag ASV scans distinctly from internal VM. (2) Join open findings `severity>=3` on same `fqdn`. (3) Alert if `fail_pct>0` two quarters in a row. (4) Store PDF reports in GRC with Splunk deep link.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:was" earliest=-120d
| where match(scan_name,"(?i)ASV|PCI\\s*External)")
| eval failed_scan=if(match(status,"(?i)error|failed|aborted"),1,0)
| timechart span=30d sum(failed_scan) as failed_runs, count as total_runs
| eval fail_pct=if(total_runs>0, round(100*failed_runs/total_runs,2), null())
```

Understanding this SPL

**ASV External Scan Failure and Non-Compliant Finding Trend (PCI DSS Req 11.3.1, 11.3.2)** — Tracks approved scanning vendor run outcomes and residual high-risk findings on Internet-facing payment hosts so quarterly external vulnerability scanning evidence stays current and remediated.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:was` or `tenable:io:vm` with `scan_type="ASV"` if tagged. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm; **sourcetype**: qualys:was. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, sourcetype="qualys:was", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(scan_name,"(?i)ASV|PCI\\s*External)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **failed_scan** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=30d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest span=30d | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**ASV External Scan Failure and Non-Compliant Finding Trend (PCI DSS Req 11.3.1, 11.3.2)** — Tracks approved scanning vendor run outcomes and residual high-risk findings on Internet-facing payment hosts so quarterly external vulnerability scanning evidence stays current and remediated.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:was` or `tenable:io:vm` with `scan_type="ASV"` if tagged. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line (`fail_pct`), column (failed_runs), table (last scan status by fqdn).
grandmaExplanation: We track approved scanning vendor run outcomes and residual high-risk findings on Internet-facing payment hosts so quarterly external vulnerability scanning evidence stays current and remediated. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Vulnerabilities (CIM data model).
